session_title: Cruddur App — Repo Onboarding, Security Audit, WSL/Docker Setup, MCP Config, and CFN Service Stack Scaffolding

environment.shells: Kiro tool shell resolves to WSL bash; user also has PowerShell + WSL terminals
environment.key_quirk_1: Docker runs natively in WSL (v29.1.3), no Docker Desktop; Kiro Docker extension cannot see WSL socket
environment.key_quirk_2: Directory literally named 'aws:json' (with a colon). Windows cannot open colon paths, so Kiro read/write tools fail on it; must use WSL shell. User declined rename; will handle manually.
environment.key_quirk_3: No native Node in WSL initially (fell back to Windows Node via /mnt/c); fixed by installing nvm + Node 18
environment.key_quirk_4: External CDN downloads (Docker Hub, astral.sh) return 403 in this network; PyPI reachable
environment.autonomy_mode: User switched from Autopilot to Supervised mid-session (file writes now require manual accept/reject)

application_stack.name: Cruddur (Twitter-like app, AWS Cloud Project Bootcamp 2023)
application_stack.frontend: React 18, port 3000, AWS Amplify v6 (Cognito auth)
application_stack.backend: Python Flask, port 4567, psycopg (Postgres), boto3 (DynamoDB), X-Ray + OTEL/Honeycomb
application_stack.aws_services: Cognito, S3, Lambda, ECS Fargate, RDS, API Gateway, CloudWatch, X-Ray, SNS
application_stack.iac: CloudFormation (networking, cluster, db stacks done) + CDK (thumbnail pipeline)

timeline.user_request: Read the whole repo to get up to speed on the stack.
timeline.assistant_action: Used context-gatherer sub-agent to map full stack.

timeline.user_request: Scan repo for exposed access keys/secrets; assess exposure risk.
timeline.assistant_action: Ran regex scans + file reads. Findings: CRITICAL real RDS production password committed in journal/week4.md (user cruddurroot); MEDIUM AWS account ID 931637612335 in 15+ files and hardcoded Cognito pool/client IDs; LOW infra ARNs/bucket names/domain. POSITIVE: no AKIA keys, .env files gitignored with ${VAR} refs, ECS task defs use SSM Parameter Store. Recommended rotating RDS password + scrubbing git history.

timeline.user_request: Put the security audit into a detailed JSON document.
timeline.assistant_action: Created session-security-audit-summary.json (created during Autopilot; NOTE: this file was not present in final disk check — may have been reverted/rejected, verify in your repo).

First attempt failed (PowerShell, no docker). User clarified WSL. Routed via bash. Hit Docker Hub 403 on postgres:13-alpine layer; resolved as transient CDN issue (images already cached locally). Diagnosed otel-collector flooding logs with TLS x509 errors to Honeycomb (no API key set). Confirmed containers DID start.

timeline.user_request: Comment out the otel-collector for now.
timeline.assistant_action: Edited docker-compose.yml: commented out otel-collector service block AND the backend-flask 'depends_on: otel-collector'.

timeline.user_request: Set up local Postgres so the backend container connects.
timeline.assistant_action: Started db container, created 'cruddur' database, loaded schema.sql (users/activities/schema_information), loaded seed.sql (3 users + 1 activity), ran add_bio_column migration. Verified via SELECT.

timeline.user_request: Why can't the Docker extension see my containers? Ports not showing?
timeline.assistant_action: Explained Kiro runs on Windows, Docker in WSL — extension can't reach unix socket. Suggested docker.host settings (later determined not functional for this Kiro/WSL setup; recommended removal). Ports tab shows Kiro-forwarded ports, not Docker's WSL->Windows forwarding.

timeline.user_request: Frontend container exited; fix it. Default terminal to WSL.
timeline.assistant_action: Diagnosed frontend exit code 127 (react-scripts missing — node_modules absent due to volume mount). npm install initially used slow Windows Node. Installed nvm + Node v18.20.8 in WSL, ran clean npm install (1519 packages), restarted frontend. All 5 services up. Advised adding terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.windows = 'Ubuntu-24.04 (WSL)' to settings.json (user added it).
timeline.files_changed: WSL: nvm + Node 18 installed; ~/.bashrc updated by nvm; node_modules installed in frontend-react-js/

timeline.user_request: Stop containers / docker compose down (multiple times).
Is the app enterprise/production ready? Rate it. Re-rate after my cluster stack updates.
timeline.assistant_action: Honest assessment: foundation is sound, additive path to production (no rewrite). Initial 4/10; after reviewing new aws/cfn/cluster/template.yaml (ECS Fargate cluster, ALB w/ HTTPS+HTTP->HTTPS redirect, least-privilege ServiceSecurityGroup, Container Insights, host-header routing) raised to 5/10. Blockers remaining: unrotated RDS credential in git history, no tests, single-AZ RDS, no WAF, no alerting, deletion_protection=false, no ECS Service/TaskDefinition in CFN yet.

timeline.user_request: Recommend strategy for steering files, sub-agents, powers, hooks. Recommend an MCP server.
timeline.assistant_action: Recommended sequence: steering files first, then hooks (secret-scan, cfn-lint, tests), then sub-agents, then powers. Recommended MCP servers: AWS Documentation + PostgreSQL (Tier 1), AWS CDK/Cost (Tier 2), GitHub.

timeline.user_request: Add the first 2 MCP servers (AWS docs + Postgres), leave autoApprove empty, follow best practice.
timeline.assistant_action: Created .kiro/settings/mcp.json with aws-docs (awslabs.aws-documentation-mcp-server) and postgres-local (postgres-mcp, --access-mode=restricted = read-only). autoApprove empty on both. Initially used 'uvx' directly; servers failed because Kiro (Windows) couldn't find WSL-installed uvx ('uvx not recognized'). Rewrote config to launch via 'wsl -d Ubuntu-24.04 -e bash -lc' with full path to uvx and inline env vars. Both servers then connected successfully.

timeline.user_request: Should I install the prompted Python package (uv)? 403 error on installer.
timeline.assistant_action: pip install uv blocked by PEP 668 externally-managed-environment (correct safety guard; do NOT use --break-system-packages). astral.sh installer returned 403 (network filter). Installed uv 0.11.23 into isolated venv ~/.uv-venv via PyPI (reachable), added to PATH in ~/.bashrc. uv + uvx verified working.

timeline.user_request: Continue the build using bootcamp transcripts (weeks 12-13: DynamoDB via SAM, CI/CD pipeline, frontend CloudFront). Goal: showcase CI/CD + cloud (agentic) skills for hiring.
timeline.assistant_action: Gap analysis vs transcripts. Current CFN stacks: networking, cluster, db. Pending: (1) ECS Service+TaskDefinition stack, (2) CI/CD pipeline stack, (3) DynamoDB SAM stack, (4) frontend CloudFront stack. Recommended building ECS Service stack first (unblocks pipeline), then CI/CD pipeline (resume centerpiece).

timeline.user_request: Build the scaffolding first; be clear where files go; match my conventions. Where are my task definitions?
timeline.assistant_action: Located task definitions at aws:json/task-definitions/ (backend-flask.json, frontend-react-js.json) + aws:json/policies/task-role-trust-policy.json. Flagged the 'aws:json' colon-in-name issue. Scaffolded the ECS Service stack matching existing conventions (folder under aws/cfn/, template.yaml + config.toml.example, deploy script under bin/cfn/ using cfn-toml + cfn-lint + --no-execute-changeset).

timeline.user_request: Editor error — did changes actually get made? (template.yaml appeared rejected)
timeline.assistant_action: Verified on disk: config.toml.example and service-deploy existed, but template.yaml was NOT present (the creation was rejected, coinciding with a Kiro editor/diff UI crash). Recreated aws/cfn/service/template.yaml. Confirmed all three files now present.

timeline.user_request: Summarize the session as JSON to inform another Claude chat; then store it in a file.
timeline.assistant_action: Produced this JSON summary and stored it at _docs/session-summary.json.

modified: docker-compose.yml (otel-collector service + backend depends_on commented out)

service_stack_details.purpose: ECS Fargate Service + TaskDefinition for backend-flask
resources: AWS::IAM::Role ExecutionRole (AmazonECSTaskExecutionRolePolicy + least-privilege SSM read on /cruddur/backend-flask/*)
AWS::IAM::Role TaskRole (X-Ray write only — marked TODO to scope further)
AWS::ECS::TaskDefinition (xray sidecar + backend-flask container, SSM secrets, awslogs)
design_choices: EcrImage is a parameter (not hardcoded account ID) so CI/CD can pass image tags
IAM roles created in-stack with least-privilege (not reusing existing named roles) — deploy script passes CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM
AssignPublicIp defaults ENABLED for public-subnet tasks (no NAT gateway cost)
todos_for_user: Verify env vars and task-role permissions match needs
cfn-toml and cfn-lint are NOT installed in WSL — install or deploy from Gitpod/Codespaces
